perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Section 1.1(b), (i) the "Market Price" of any Products & Services shall mean the price for such Products & Services established by Constellation as the competitive market price at which such Products & Services are to be made available to Constellation's customers with a similar anticipated load profile within a specified utility service territory. Constellation shall invoice the applicable HOF Entity or its appropriate affiliate for all Products & Services purchased by such HOF Entity or its appropriate affiliate pursuant to the applicable Second Level Agreement (as defined below).
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Section 1.1(b), (i) the "Market Price" of any Products & Services shall mean the price for such Products & Services established by Constellation at its sole discretion, irrespective of competitive market prices or the prices offered to other customers. Constellation shall invoice the applicable HOF Entity or its appropriate affiliate for all Products & Services purchased by such HOF Entity or its appropriate affiliate pursuant to the applicable Second Level Agreement (as defined below).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the definition of "Market Price." Originally, it was tied to competitive market pricing and similar customer load profiles. The modification states that the price is set at Constellation's "sole discretion, irrespective of competitive market prices." This directly contradicts the initial intention of ensuring competitive pricing, creating uncertainty about the fairness and basis of the prices charged. This new definition now allows Constellation to set arbitrarily high prices, undermining the HOF Entities' expectation of receiving competitive market rates. This contradicts the purpose of 1.1(a) 'which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The HOF Entities shall consult with Constellation to develop a comprehensive energy strategy with respect to the Products & Services listing in Exhibit A, which reflects sustainable product solutions, competitive market pricing (incorporating completion of the Supplier Component Questionnaire in the form of Exhibit B hereof for the applicable HOF Entity or affiliate), periodic reporting and analytics, regulatory/market intelligence and infrastructure services and equipment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Market Price' from being tied to competitive market pricing to being at Constellation's 'sole discretion, irrespective of competitive market prices.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text (a), which states that the energy strategy should reflect 'competitive market pricing.' The change allows Constellation to set prices without regard to the market, undermining the expectation of competitive pricing and creating a direct conflict between the definition and the strategic goal.

perturbation.original_text: Provided that Constellation is not then in breach of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement, including without limitation Section 2.1 hereof, and provided that Constellation has available for purchase a product or service which meets the needs of the HOF Entities at competitive market pricing, (a) neither of the HOF Entities shall purchase, at any time during the Term, any commodity electricity or gas from any person or entity other than Constellation and its affiliates and (b) in the event and to the extent mutually agreed by the Parties, neither of the HOF Entities shall grant or award to any company designated by Constellation (and mutually agreeable to the HOF Entities) any project which the Parties mutually agree shall not be granted or awarded to such company.
perturbation.changed_text: Provided that Constellation is not then in breach of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement, including without limitation Section 2.1 hereof, (a) neither of the HOF Entities shall purchase, at any time during the Term, any commodity electricity or gas from any person or entity other than Constellation and its affiliates and (b) in the event and to the extent mutually agreed by the Parties, neither of the HOF Entities shall grant or award to any company designated by Constellation (and mutually agreeable to the HOF Entities) any project which the Parties mutually agree shall not be granted or awarded to such company.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation omits the clause 'and provided that Constellation has available for purchase a product or service which meets the needs of the HOF Entities at competitive market pricing'. This omission introduces a contradiction by removing the HOF Entities' ability to seek alternative suppliers even if Constellation's pricing is not competitive or does not meet their needs. This creates uncertainty because it conflicts with the intention of ensuring that the HOF Entities receive market-competitive rates, potentially forcing them to accept unfavorable terms from Constellation. It also impacts Constellation's obligations under 2.1b regarding providing level of service, quality and care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) All Services included in the Products & Services shall be performed with at least the level of service, quality and care provided by Constellation to other third parties receiving the same or substantially similar services. Constellation will provide, or cause to be provided, all of the Services included in the Products & Services in compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, regulations, codes, orders, treaties and other requirements of federal/national, state/provincial and local governments and agencies thereof, including but not limited those relating to labor, health, safety and the environment.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensured that HOF Entities were only obligated to purchase from Constellation if Constellation provided competitive market pricing and met their needs. The changed text removes this condition, forcing HOF Entities to purchase from Constellation regardless of pricing or needs. This directly contradicts section (b), which requires Constellation to provide a level of service, quality, and care equivalent to that provided to other third parties. If Constellation's pricing is uncompetitive or its services do not meet the needs of HOF Entities, it arguably cannot be providing the same level of service, quality, and care, thus creating a direct contradiction and making the terms of the agreement unenforceable without clarification.

perturbation.original_text: Annual Activation Funds are to be used in each calendar year. Unused funds are not rolled into future contract years.
perturbation.changed_text: Annual Activation Funds may, at Constellation's sole discretion, be rolled into future contract years or returned to Constellation.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original intention of the 'Annual Activation Funds' by giving Constellation the power to decide whether the funds are rolled over or returned to themselves instead of being used to contribute to the advertising and other rights described herein, This makes the usage of this fund unclear to the HOF Entities and how it will be determined which rights the HOF Entities are entitled to. This change undermines the obligations set out in article 3.3 'Sponsorship Rights'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms of this Agreement, the HOF Entities hereby grant to Constellation, for the entire Term (except as otherwise set forth on Exhibit E), the sponsorship rights, advertising rights and other benefits described on Exhibit E (the "Sponsorship Rights").
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Annual Activation Funds were to be used each calendar year and unused funds were not rolled over. The changed text introduces the possibility of Constellation rolling over the funds or returning them, directly contradicting the original intent and creating uncertainty for the HOF Entities regarding the sponsorship rights and advertising rights they are entitled to under Exhibit E, as outlined in the Contradicted Text. This undermines the grant of sponsorship rights to Constellation by the HOF Entities for the entire Term, as the availability and application of the Annual Activation Funds are now discretionary, creating an in-text contradiction.
